body.title: Remove or drastically extend the 300s timeout on local reading extraction — honor 'slow on slow is ok'
body.result: Removed urllib.urlopen timeouts on book_learner._extract_nodes_local AND after_action_reviewer._call_ollama. Both were clipping on cold qwen2.5:7b CPU starts. Principle: slow-on-slow-is-ok; hang safety will come from worker-pool layer later. Commit 698625ea.
body.description:
Discovered 2026-04-19 12:26 via smoke test on real URL (Akien's blog post). ALL three chunks returned 0 nodes with summary='local inference error: timed out'. qwen2.5:7b on CPU under CC load consistently takes more than 300s per chunk — the urllib.request.urlopen timeout in book_learner._extract_nodes_local() silently clips the call and returns empty.

Directly contradicts Akien's stated principle (2026-04-18): 'we put things that can be slow on the slow resources (local when possible, no timeouts, slow is ok).' The 300s timeout is a load-bearing policy violation that produces silent failures no amount of prompt-tuning fixes.

## The fix
`book_learner._extract_nodes_local()` line ~481: `urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=300)`.

Option A: remove the timeout entirely (`urlopen(req)` — no timeout = wait forever). Matches Akien's principle directly. Risk: if qwen hangs, the worker hangs. Mitigated because we're moving to a worker-pool (T-reading-worker-pool) where the pool manager can kill a truly-hung worker.

Option B: raise to 1800s (30 min) per call. Practical middle ground. Still caps the worst case but stops clipping legitimately-slow extractions.

Lean: **A if T-reading-worker-pool lands first** (worker-pool provides the hang guard at a higher level). **B as standalone fix** until the worker-pool lands.

## Also check the other timeouts
- after_action_reviewer._call_ollama() has a 45s timeout — already flagged today as too short for cold qwen starts
- inference_gateway and cluster_router _probe timeouts — shorter probes are OK (3s), they're for health checks not actual work

Standard: ANY call labeled 'training' or 'reading' or 'bulk' should have no timeout OR a very generous one. Interactive calls can keep shorter timeouts for UX.

## Acceptance
- Smoke test against /tmp/run_smoke_read.py (or equivalent) completes with ≥1 node per chunk on at least one block
- No extract-diag line saying 'local inference error: timed out' for a block that would complete given more time
- Re-run on trainmymonkey post that timed out today produces real deposits

## Related
- T-reading-audit-qwen-complete (sibling — prompt/filter/gate fixes that all work but were masked by this)
- T-reading-worker-pool (once it lands, timeout removal is safer)
- T-extract-prompt-zero-tolerance (completed, now unmasked and testable)
- after_action_reviewer timeout (flagged 2026-04-18, separate)

## Provenance
Akien 2026-04-19 smoke test with new extract-diag logger revealed timeout as the true cause of all recent zero-node results.
